prompt: Authentic Edo-period Japanese woodblock print (ukiyo-e). A sweeping, dramatic landscape of rolling hills and a deep central valley basin. The terrain is defined by highly stylized, curving, concentric contour ridges in the earth, resembling a topographical map's elevation bands. A small traditional traveler figure is positioned on the steep slope, taking a precise, calculated step perpendicularly downward across the contour lines, heading directly toward the lowest, deepest point of the valley. Flat areas of unmodulated color, Prussian blue, muted ochre, and vermilion. Visible woodgrain texture, stark black ink outlines. Asymmetrical composition. --no calligraphy, text, characters, letters, red seals, stamps, modern elements, charts, diagrams, holograms, 3d render, shading
tradition_approach: Applies ukiyo-e's stylized topographical contour ridges, flat unmodulated color fields, and Prussian blue palette to depict a traveler stepping perpendicularly across elevation bands toward the deepest valley minimum, mirroring a loss landscape.
